revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[Unicode]] [[Tangut script|Tangut]] [[font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing programmatically generated pages
1 revision imported: Mass importing wikipedia categories, not taking histories because these are unused ones and it is kind of obvious where they came from
Adding [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[Unicode]] [[Tangut script|Tangut]] [[font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[Unicode]] [[Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]] [[Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]] [[:en:Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]] [[:en:Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]] [[:en:Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]] [[:en:Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23626449376|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tangut/fonts.css}} === Usage === {{Tangut|}} {{Tangut||}} {{Tangut|||}} This applies a [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]] [[:en:Tangut script|Tangut]] [[:en:Font|Font]] to the specified Tangut text if such a font is available on the user's system. The fonts applied are, in the following order: * '''Tangut N4694''' — Tangut font based on Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}) and Jia Changye ({{lang|zh-Hans|贾常业}})'s work (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#n4694]) * '''Tangut Yinchuan''' — Tangut font originally designed by Jing Yongshi ({{lang|zh-Hans|景永时}}), but extended and modified by Andrew West and Michael Everson to cover all Unicode Tangut ideographs and components (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Yinchuan.html]) * '''New Tangut Std''' or '''New Tangut''' — Tangut font based on characters found in Tangut dictionaries as ''Sea of Characters'' ({{lang|zh|文海}}), ''Homophones'' ({{lang|zh|同音}}), etc. (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#nt]) * '''Tangut TWU''' — Academia Sinica's remapped Tangut font (available from [http://ccamc.co/fonts.php#tt]) * '''TH-Tshyn-P1''' — freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''TH-Feon-B''' — another freeware font of the {{lang|zh-Hans|字海(叶典)}} online Chinese character dictionary (available from [http://yedict.com/download/]) * '''BabelStone Tangut Wenhai''' — freeware font that covers 3,061 of the 6,125 Tangut ideographs (available from [http://www.babelstone.co.uk/Fonts/Wenhai.html]); ===Parameters=== The first parameter is the Unicode Tangut text to be displayed. The second parameter is the transliteration or translation of the Tangut text, which will be displayed as the mouseover tooltip. This is optional, and if omitted the tooltip will be "Tangut text". The third parameter is the font size to be used in px units. This is optional, and if omitted the default font size will be used. ===Examples=== #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty}} #{{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} provides {{Tangut|𗴂𗹭𘜶𗴲𗂧|Great Xia State of the White and the Lofty|size=24}} === See also === {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Writing system templates]] [[Category:Script–font templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
